celebration: The Duck joins the cast · ck = one sound, two letters

songs.title: K-K-Kick
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
k… k… k…
(dry clicks, like a clock)

[Hook 1 — kids chant]
K! K! K-k-k!
What begins with K?
K-k-kite! K-k-kite!
It's a kite! (a kite!)

[Hook 2 — kids chant]
K! K! K-k-k!
What begins with K?
K-k-king! K-k-king!
It's a king! (a king!)

[Hook 3 — kids chant]
K! K! K-k-k!
What begins with K?
K-k-key! K-k-key!
It's a key! (a key!)

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
k… k… k…
k and c… the same click… k… c… k…
duck… sock… rock… (the same click at the end!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not K!
(k… k… k…)

[Final Hook — big]
K! K! K-k-k!
K-k-kangaroo! K-k-kangaroo!
It's a kangaroo, kangaroo, kangaroo!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The K sound is a crisp unvoiced /k/ click — identical to Week 4's C. NEVER 'kay' (letter name) and NEVER 'kuh' (schwa). The breakdown teaches the year's biggest spelling secret: k and c make the SAME click, and at the END of a short word we write it 'ck' (duck, sock, rock). Those '-ck' words are said as ONE click, never two. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: The Duck Got the Sock
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(quack… quack…)

[Hook]
The duck got the sock!
The duck got the sock!
What got the sock? (the duck! the duck!)
The duck got the sock!

[Verse 2]
The duck got the rock!
The duck got the rock!
What got the rock? (the duck! the duck!)
The duck got the rock!

[Verse 3]
The duck got the kit!
The duck got the kit!
Kick! The duck can kick!
(get it back! get it back!)

[Verse 4]
The kid got the duck!
The kid got the duck!
Back! Get back, duck!
(quack! quack!)

[Outro]
The duck got the potato!
(POTATO!)
The duck got the sock…
(quack… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: One frame: 'The duck got the ___!' — the target swaps per verse (sock → rock → kit → potato). Every '-ck' word (duck, sock, rock, kick, back) is said as ONE click. The duck is the new cast member — cheeky, quick, a thief. Kids choir answers (the duck! the duck!).

spreads.artNote: The Duck's grand entrance — a plump white duck mid-waddle, one bright eye on the reader, plotting something. Cast member joins.
The Duck triumphantly holding a stripey sock in its bill, head high.
The Duck straining to lift a rock nearly its own size, tail up, determined.
The Duck sitting on a little first-aid kit / pencil kit it has stolen, guarding it.
The Duck mid-kick, one webbed foot flung out, sending the sock flying. Action pose.

spreads.text: The cat ran. The duck ran!
spreads.artNote: The Cat (tabby) fleeing with the sock; the Duck in hot pursuit right behind, both mid-stride. Chaos.
A small child (a 'kid') scooping up the wriggling Duck in both arms, both surprised. Sock still in the Duck's bill.
The Duck waddling backward toward the reader, wings up, refusing to give the sock back. Comedy standoff.
The Duck sitting on the potato like an egg, spotlit. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS the word from the picture. The Duck looks smug.

extras: The ck secret: sandpaper c + sandpaper k pinned together as ONE tile — build du-ck, so-ck, ro-ck, ki-ck and read each -ck as a single click
K vs C sort: k-words (kite, king, key) beside c-words (cat, cup, car) — same sound, note which start with which
Duck puppet joins the cast basket beside Segina, Sam, Cat, Ant, Dog, Rat and Bug — count them: seven friends now

REUSED FROM WEEK 1 — a potato wearing tiny round glasses standing at a miniature whiteboard with a pointer, teaching, deadpan, ultra-realistic photograph, single subject centered, dramatic spotlight on deep forest-green backdrop, soft shadows, cinematic, slightly whimsical --ar 3:2 --style raw

teacherFocus: Two lessons in one week. First: K makes the exact same click as C — sort k-words and c-words, same sound, different letter. Second, THE ck SECRET: at the end of a short word the /k/ click is spelled 'ck' (duck, sock, rock, kick) and read as ONE sound, never two. Pin sandpaper c+k together as a single tile. The Duck joins the cast as the cheeky thief — seven friends now.
